title: T-011 — Supabase Auth (GoTrue) wiring with @supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs and 15-min access / 60-min idle
prompt:
# T-011 — Supabase Auth (GoTrue) wiring with @supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs and 15-min access / 60-min idle

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Configure Supabase Auth as the primary IdP per §5.x: 15-min access token, rotating refresh, 60-min idle, 8-h absolute, magic-link 7-day TTL.

## Implementation contract
Add @supabase/supabase-js and @supabase/auth-helpers-nextjs to apps/web. Create lib/auth/supabase-server.ts and lib/auth/supabase-browser.ts factories. Configure access TTL=15min, refresh rotation enabled, idle=60min (read from tenant_idp_config), session_max=8h. Implement Server Action signInWithMagicLink(email) that generates a signed 7-day TTL token. Add middleware.ts validating the session JWT and establishing the non-spoofable org context by calling `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` (T-007). RLS policies read the resulting context via the foundation function `app.current_org_id()` from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC. Vitest unit tests on the helpers with a mocked Supabase client. Playwright E2E covering the magic-link round-trip against a Supabase local instance.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/lib/auth/supabase-server.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/auth/supabase-browser.ts [create]
- apps/web/middleware.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/(auth)/actions.ts [create]
- apps/web/__tests__/auth/magic-link.e2e.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/package.json [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a Supabase local instance is running, when signInWithMagicLink('user@example.com') is called and the resulting token is consumed within 7 days, then a session is established and the access token TTL header is 15 minutes
2. Given a signed-in user has been idle 61 minutes, when the next request hits middleware.ts, then it returns 401 and forces re-authentication
3. Given a successful sign-in and middleware has called app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id), when the request reaches a server action, then app.current_org_id() (the foundation function) resolves to the user's org UUID and RLS-scoped SELECTs see exactly that org's rows

## Test strategy
- RED: write apps/web/__tests__/auth/magic-link.e2e.test.ts asserting the 15-min/60-min/8-h windows before middleware.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test:e2e -- magic-link'

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not set or trust spoofable app.tenant_id; resolve and set non-spoofable org context via `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` per T-007.
- Do not store JWT secrets in the repo
- Do not bypass app.set_org_context in middleware — RLS depends on it

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-001
- T-010

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Configure Supabase Auth as the primary IdP per §5.x: 15-min access token, rotating refresh, 60-min idle, 8-h absolute, magic-link 7-day TTL. Middleware establishes non-spoofable org context via app.set_org_context().
